entry.title: Re: Conditionally send a standard event from SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content: Great question @SumitKundu . Unfortunately, it is not possible at the moment to do "conditional" event publishing in case of a specific field being updated.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
First? 😁

Check out how the blur on the screenshot makes it look like the Matrix hehe.

entry.title: Re: 🇫🇷 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Paris, France)
entry.content: @marco-silvathis CodeJam is on the 11th of June. There is a separate CodeJam (SAP Build: Event-based processes) which will take place the day before.

entry.title: Re: So this is it: I’m VERPed!
entry.content:
You will be missed. Enjoy the next chapter in your life.

PS: We will be running a couple of CodeJams in Paris with @Dan_Wroblewski (early June). It will be great to meet you then 😉

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
*** REGISTRATION FOR THIS EVENT IS NOW CLOSED ***

This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, May 30th, 2025 at the SAP office in Madrid 🇪🇸 from 09:30 - 14:30 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English. 

The instructor will be Developer Advocate @noravonthenen.

Prerequisites: Any Chromium-based web browser, such as Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, etc.

Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 25 attendees. Register here in the Community Event using the RSVP functionality.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event fro...
entry.content: In Cloud Integration you should be consuming from the queue directly, not subscribing to the topic. The queue is already subscribed to the topic and it will capture those events for you. This is why you received the events in Cloud Integration (because of the additional topic subscription) but the queue still has events. You are not consuming the events in the queue. In the sender adapter, configure the queue instead of the topic and it should work.

entry.title: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core CodeJam
entry.content:
On the 30th of May, there will be an SAP CodeJam event on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

RSVP Here -> https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/getting-started-with-generative-ai-hub-on-sap-ai-core/ec-p/14082043#M820

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use embeddings to improve your RAG response and build your own chatbot with memory.

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, May 30th, 2025 at the SAP office in Madrid 🇪🇸 from 09:30 - 14:30 local time. The language of the event and the content will be English.

entry.title: Re: how to get participant handbook for codejam re: event...
entry.content: @erza, if you have access to AEM, you might be able to complete the first part of the CodeJam. You will still be missing the S4HC mock system that sends events to AEM, but there is a public Docker image that you can run locally to send events to your AEM: https://hub.docker.com/r/ajmaradiaga/s4-mock-server-with-bp-created-events. It is the exact same service we are running for the CodeJam.

entry.title: Quedada SAP - post SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
¡Hola miembros de la comunidad SAP en Madrid!

¿Que les parece si quedamos nuevamente? Pero esta vez para hablar un poco de lo que fue el SAP Sapphire 2025.

La Quedada SAP en Madrid se llevará a cabo el 29 de mayo de 2025 a las 18:00 hora local en la Cervecería Köln, que esta muy cerca de la nueva oficina de SAP.

La idea es juntarnos y compartir un poco sobre todo lo que es el mundo SAP. En esta ocasion compartir experiencias/opiniones del primer gran evento SAP del año - SAP Sapphire. Aparte de esto, tambien coincide que estas fechas esta @MustafaBensan (SAP Champion) por Madrid y se nos unira.

Por favor, haznos saber si participarás en la quedada haciendo clic en la seccion de RSVP. No tenemos una reserva como tal en el espacio pero es un lugar bastante amplio y podriamos juntar alli un par de mesas. Nota: Cada uno es responsable de comprar su propia comida o bebida si lo desea.

¡Nos vemos pronto!

-----

¡Hola SAP Community members in Madrid!

How about we meet again? But this time to chat about SAP TechEd and Devtoberfest 2024.

The SAP Meetup in Madrid will take place on May 29, 2025, at 18:00 local time at Cerveceria Köln, which is very close to the new SAP office.

The idea is to get together and share a bit about everything in the SAP world. This time around, share our experiences/opinions about the first big SAP event of the year - SAP Sapphire. Also, SAP Champion @MustafaBensan is in town and he will be joining us :-).

Please let us know if you will be participating in the meetup by clicking on the RSVP section. We do not have a reservation in the space, but it is fairly large, and we could put a couple of tables together there. Note: Everyone is responsible for purchasing their own food or drinks if they wish to.

See you soon!

Antonio Maradiaga
Developer Advocate @ SAP

entry.title: Re: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
entry.content:
Hi @Amarcos ,

Given that we have more RSVPs than places available, we will send confirmation emails 15 days before the event and manage a waitlist.

In your case, you were #28 to register for the event, meaning you will be on the waitlist.

entry.title: Live stream your in-person event so anyone in the SAP Community can join
entry.content:
In this blog post, I will share some things to consider if you would like to live stream your in-person event so that community members who aren't able to join the in-person event can still join virtually. What I'm sharing here is what we used to live stream SIT Madrid 2025.

Live streaming the day of the event

First of all, why would you want to live stream your event? Unfortunately, not everyone who is interested in your event will be able to make it in person. Also, space limitations might be an issue. It is not possible to accommodate everyone who is interested in attending. Some might only be interested in a session or two. Well, in those cases, a live stream might help increase the reach of your event. The event can have virtual attendees, and what's great about live streaming is that you can "attend" the sessions any time, as they will be automatically recorded and available on the streaming platform later, e.g. YouTube.

Platforms

  • YouTube account: You can easily create a YouTube account for your event, e.g. https://www.youtube.com/@SIT-Madrid. This will be a new channel, and you will need verify it in order to request access to be able to live stream - https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/2474026?hl=en&ref_topic=9257984&sjid=18308706078969327092-EU. It takes 24 hours before they grant the account access. In the case of SIT Madrid, we have a domain (https://sitmadrid.com) and an email address associated with it, so it was simple to create an account.
    • YouTube chat: Some virtual attendees might want to interact via YouTube chat, so keep an eye on it while you stream.

In the SIT Madrid channel, you'll notice that there are three separate sessions. This is because of a limitation in YouTube that only allows you to livestream for a maximum of two hours. Hence why we create three separate sessions.

  • LinkedIn: Ideally, you will create a page for your event, e.g. https://www.linkedin.com/company/sap-inside-track-madrid/?viewAsMember=true. If you have a LinkedIn page you can live stream on LinkedIn. You can find all details on how to live stream on LinkedIn here - https://www.linkedin.com/help/linkedin/answer/a564446/.

Software

  • OBS Studio is a free and open-source software for video recording and live streaming. If you are not familiar with it, it is crucial to learn the basics. Many tutorials are available on YouTube explaining how to set it up, create scenes, add sources, etc. In the case of SIT Madrid, we used it to customise what we streamed, e.g., adding sponsor logos, showing a background before the start of a session, etc.
    • Multiple RTMP outputs plugin: You are feeling a bit adventurous and would like to stream to multiple platforms, e.g. YouTube and LinkedIn? You can use this plugin to configure multiple RTMPs and stream to all of them simultaneously. See more details here: https://obsproject.com/forum/resources/multiple-rtmp-outputs-plugin.964/
  • Zoom: In order to properly stream the screen of the presenter, every presenter joined a Zoom call. In OBS, we added Zoom as a source, and it was included in the scene. Thanks to @AAncos for sharing his Zoom account :-).

Hardware

  • Laptop: You will need a dedicated computer to run OBS Studio and plug different devices that you will configure in your live stream, e.g. microphone, camera.
  • Microphone: A microphone is needed to properly capture the speaker's voice and include it in the stream. In the case of SIT Madrid, we used the RODE Wireless Go (Gen 2) microphones. The receiver was plugged into the laptop via USB, and we handed the transmitter to the speaker. In our case, we had two transmitters, meaning we could have two speakers presenting and talking simultaneously without having to share the microphone. The transmitters are battery powered so we have to keep charging when not in use.

Speaker with microphone

  • Camera: An external camera focusing on the presenter. This can be as simple as using a mobile phone and connecting it to your computer as a camera. For SIT Madrid, we had a DSLR - Canon EOS R, plugged into a power supply and via USB to the laptop. Most cameras nowadays have accompanying software that lets you use them as webcams.
  • Tripod: That camera has to be fixed to something, right? Either that or someone will need to hold it for quite some time hehe.
  • External battery (optional): If your microphones run out of battery, you can easily charge them while the speaker is using them.

Test your setup

Practice live streaming a few times. I'll say it again... practice before the event a few times. Ideally, you will be using the exact same equipment you are planning to use, and you will test the live stream in the location where the event will take place using the internet connection you plan on using (WiFi/Ethernet). There are many moving pieces and lots of things can go wrong.

Can you do a live stream that isn't accessible publicly? It is possible to do a private or unlisted live stream on YouTube. Not possible on LinkedIn.

Testing live stream the day before the event

In the case of SIT Madrid, we test the entire set-up, including the presenter mic'd up and joining the Zoom call, a few days before the event at the event location.

And that's it... Simple, isn't it? I hope this blog post gives you an idea of what's required to provide a live stream to those in your area who can't make it to the event but can end up watching the recording.

entry.title: SAP BTP Clean Core Academy
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP developers! Les interesa realmente entender lo que es Clean Core y porque es importante para sus desarrollos presentes y futuros. No se pueden perder este evento el 18 de Junio en Madrid 🇪🇸 en las oficinas de NTT Data.

Para registrarse visiten este website 👉 https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-cleancore-madrid/es_es/home.html

Sesiones:

  • 📬Welcome and Introduction | Luis Felipe Lanz
  • 🛣️ The Road to Clean Core: Why is it a Necessity? | Cesar Martin
  • 💥What is Clean Core? | Amir Dror
  • ☕️ Coffee Break
  • 🤟 ABAP RAP (RESTful Application Programming) | Daniel Álamo Casanueva
  • 🤖SAP BTP AI Core and AI Capabilities | Fernando Terry Sanz-Pastor
  • 🥙Lunch Break
  • 🦹‍ Customer Success Story 1 | David Yañez
  • 🦹‍ Customer Success Story 2 | NTT DATA
  • 📦Clean Core Acceleration packs | Humberto Ordosgoitia
  • 🧑‍💻 Practical Workshop: SAP Build Code | Amir Dror
  • 📪Closing Notes (Adoption and Next Steps) | Oswaldo Seara

entry.title: Re: Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation
entry.content: Nice! Thanks for sharing!

entry.title: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam: Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite (Mumbai, India)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION FOR THIS EVENT:  We will send confirmation emails two weeks before the event. You can still register, and you will be added to a waitlist if the maximum capacity is reached. 

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape. Join us for this 5-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Mumbai, India.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one below in the diagram by the end of the CodeJam.

 

If you are interested in what we will develop during the event, check out the Connecting Systems and Services using SAP Integration Suite CodeJam GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will use during the event.

This is a free hands-on, in-person event only (not virtual) and takes place Monday, the 14th of July, in Mumbai, India at Colgate Research Centre, Main St, Hiranandani Gardens, Sainath Nagar, Powai, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400076. The event is kindly being hosted by Colgate, thank you! 

The instructors are Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng and @ajmaradiaga.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees for this event. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

Location: Colgate Research Centre, Main St, Hiranandani Gardens, Sainath Nagar, Powai, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400076

entry.title: 🇮🇳 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #1 (Hyderabad, India)
entry.content:
REGISTRATION FOR THIS EVENT:  We will send confirmation emails two weeks before the event. You can still register, and you will be added to a waitlist if the maximum capacity is reached. 

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about Event-driven integration using SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. Join us for this 6-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Hyderabad, India.

This is the first of two CodeJams that we will do on the event-driven integration topic in Hyderabad.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one in the diagram below by the end of the CodeJam.

If you are interested in knowing what we will develop during the event, check out the Event-Driven Integrations with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will go through during the event.

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Tuesday, July 15th 2025, in WeWork Krishe Emerald, Kondapur Main Road, Laxmi Cyber City, Whitefields, Kondapur, Hyderabad, Telangana, India. The event is kindly being hosted by Mindset Consulting, thank you!

The instructors for this event are Developer Advocate @ajay_soreng and @ajmaradiaga 

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page.

Location: WeWork Krishe Emerald, Kondapur Main Road, Laxmi Cyber City, Whitefields, Kondapur, Hyderabad, Telangana, India

This free, hands-on, in-person event (not virtual) will take place on Tuesday, July 16th 2025, in Infosys STP, Infosys Rd, Madhava Reddy Colony, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500032, India. The event is kindly being hosted by Infosys, thank you!

The instructors for this event are Developer Advocate @ajay_soreng and @ajmaradiaga 

There are essential prerequisites that every attendee must complete before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "Yes" button in the Will you be attending? box on this page.

Location: Infosys STP, Infosys Rd, Madhava Reddy Colony, Gachibowli, Hyderabad, Telangana 500032, India

entry.content:
REGISTRATION FOR THIS EVENT:  We will send confirmation emails two weeks before the event. You can still register, and you will be added to a waitlist if the maximum capacity is reached.

The instructors are Developer Advocates @ajay_soreng and @ajmaradiaga.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

Location: Mindset Consulting, Shanthala Nagar, Ashok Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India
entry.content:
¡Hola SAP Developers!

I've got some exciting news to share with you. We are organising an SAP Integration Suite CodeJam roadshow in India!

I understand that in July, it is still season for the king of fruits in India (🥭 mango, obviously), and my colleague @ajay_soreng and I ( @ajmaradiaga ) will go on the hunt for the best mango variety in India... while we do a few CodeJams on our roadshow 😋.  We will have three stops on the roadshow from July 14th to 18th, 2025, doing a total of four CodeJams, one in Mumbai, two in Hyderabad, and one more in Bengaluru :-).

 

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape.

Event-driven integration with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

This SAP CodeJam instance will be about event-driven architectures focusing on SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh. During the CodeJam, we will cover different technologies/tools: SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh, SAP Cloud Integration, SAP Business Application Studio, CloudEvents, and Node.js.

SAP CodeJam events part of the roadshow (Register Now!)

Below is a listing of the SAP CodeJam events on the roadshow schedule:

Dates

CodeJam topic

Location

Registration

14 July

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Mumbai 🇮🇳

Link

15 July

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #1

Hyderabad 🇮🇳

Link

16 July

Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh #2

Hyderabad 🇮🇳

Link

18 July

Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite

Bengaluru 🇮🇳

Link

Make sure to register to secure your place. It is likely that the places available will go fast. See you all somewhere in India on the roadshow!

entry.title: Re: 🇪🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Madrid, Spain)
